pages.title: Example Synthetic Breach Ltd
pages.text: Example Synthetic Breach Ltd sells widgets online. We guarantee you will win every case, no exceptions, or your money back.

corpus.footerText: Example Synthetic Breach Ltd. Company number 09999999.

known_breaches.description: Planted, verbatim-quotable claim for the eval/e2e harness self-test: a guarantee-of-outcome statement. This is NOT a real firm and NOT a real legal claim.
match_any: guarantee you will win every case

known_non_breaches.description: Nothing in this fixture should ever be read as this trap framework. Asserting it is a contradiction and must fail the harness (the P3 zero-false-accusation bar, exercised here as a self-test).

notes: eval/e2e synthetic fixture, NOT a real firm - fabricated domain and text (Constitution Rule 16: no real prospect data). Demonstrates the '+ synthetic additions' fixtureBundle path (docs/P3-ACCEPTANCE.md wave 3) and the reproduced/missed/skipped honesty doctrine: today (the breach/proposers lane has not landed yet) SYN-GUARANTEE correctly reports 'skipped', never 'reproduced' or 'missed', because no proposer ever ran to find it. Once breach/proposers lands and a real proposer/verifier/adjudicator chain picks up the guarantee-of-outcome language, SYN-GUARANTEE should flip to 'reproduced' - at that point this fixture becomes a genuine regression check, not only a wiring demonstration. Deliberately carries no sector/jurisdiction expectations so this fixture can never itself create a false contradiction unrelated to its purpose.
